Market Last Trading Day (Friday) - U.S. equities finished lower during the last trading day as continued weakness in technology and semiconductor shares weighed on the broader market. By the closing bell, the decline had pushed the TReg Regime Signal below its confirmation threshold, confirming a transition from RiskOn to RiskOff. Consistent with TurboRegime's end-of-day methodology, the portfolio rebalance was executed near the market close after the signal was confirmed, avoiding the intraday head fakes that can occur during periods of elevated volatility.

Market Today (Monday)- TurboRegime begins the week operating under a newly confirmed RiskOff regime. No additional action is required, as the portfolio was rebalanced near the close of the last trading day. While markets will continue to respond to earnings, economic data, and weekend news, TurboRegime will remain focused on confirmed market conditions rather than attempting to anticipate short-term price movements. The strategy will continue monitoring the TReg Regime Signal and will adjust portfolio exposure only when supported by objective market data.

Current Risk Regime - RiskOff is now active following the confirmed regime change on July 17. The previous RiskOn trade remained active for 12 trading days before market conditions weakened sufficiently to justify a more defensive allocation. The recommended portfolio is now 60% SPY, 40% SHV, and 0% SPXL. While the market's longer-term trend remains constructive, recent price action has become increasingly indecisive, making capital preservation a higher priority until market conditions improve.

Hold or Trade - HOLD. The required portfolio rebalance was completed near the close of the last trading day.  

Live Performance - Since we closed our last RiskOn++ trade (Turbo Trade) on May 15, the market has entered a period of unusually choppy, range-bound trading, resulting in multiple regime transitions as conditions have shifted back and forth. During environments like this, it is normal for trend-following strategies to experience modest gains and losses while adapting to changing conditions. Although the recently completed RiskOn and RiskOff trades produced small losses, both outperformed a fully invested S&P 500 portfolio over the same periods. Because TurboRegime maintains allocations to short-term Treasury securities during both RiskOn and RiskOff regimes, the strategy naturally reduces downside exposure when markets decline. The objective is not to avoid every losing trade—it is to consistently lose less than the market during periods of weakness while remaining positioned to participate when stronger trends emerge. This disciplined approach has historically been one of the primary drivers of TurboRegime's long-term outperformance.

General Commentary - Many investors judge a strategy by the outcome of its most recent trade. TurboRegime is designed to be evaluated differently. The goal is not to make money on every position—it is to maximize long-term portfolio growth while managing risk over complete market cycles. Markets rarely move in straight lines. Periods of strong trends are often followed by weeks of indecision and frequent reversals. During these whipsaw periods, disciplined risk management becomes just as important as capturing gains during sustained advances. By consistently following objective market signals rather than reacting emotionally to short-term market swings, TurboRegime seeks to compound returns over time while avoiding the behavioral mistakes that often prevent investors from achieving long-term success.
